test_cases.input: Design a new interaction interface for the chating robot.

arguments.user_personas: Young adults (18-35) who frequently use messaging apps, prefer quick and intuitive UI, comfortable with emojis and GIFs.
arguments.interaction_modes: Voice commands, text chat, gesture recognition.
arguments.design_principles: Minimalist design, high contrast for readability, responsive layout, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1).
arguments.usability_testing: A/B testing with 50 users, heuristic evaluation, think-aloud protocol.
arguments.output_artifacts: Interactive prototype, persona documentation, usability test report.

tools_called.name: UIUX_Expert

args.user_personas: Primary users: general consumers aged 16-60, including first-time robot users, families, and tech-savvy users. Secondary users: accessibility-focused users who may rely on screen readers, larger text, or voice input. Goals: quick, friendly, natural conversation with minimal learning curve.
args.interaction_modes: Text chat as the default interaction mode, with optional voice input/output, quick-reply buttons, emoji/reaction shortcuts, and command chips for common tasks. Include multi-turn conversation, interruption handling, and status feedback during robot actions.
args.design_principles: Accessibility-first, conversational clarity, low cognitive load, trust and transparency, feedback-rich interaction, error tolerance, and consistent visual hierarchy. Use large touch targets, readable typography, high contrast, and clear system status indicators.
args.prototyping_tools: Figma for wireframes and interactive prototypes, with a component library for chat bubbles, quick actions, voice controls, and robot status cards.
args.usability_testing: Task-based usability testing, heuristic evaluation, first-click testing, and A/B testing for chat layout and quick-action placement. Measure task completion, time to response, error recovery, and user satisfaction.
args.output_artifacts: Interaction flow, low-fidelity wireframes, high-fidelity prototype, component library, and usability test report.

test_cases.input: Please provide a website development proposal, including website architecture, database design, and user experience optimization.

arguments.system_design: A three-tier website architecture with a presentation layer (React.js frontend), an application layer (Node.js/Express backend), and a data layer (PostgreSQL). Incorporates CDN and load balancer for scalability and performance, along with secure API endpoints and modular components for maintainability.
arguments.technology_stack: Frontend: React.js, Backend: Node.js with Express, Database: PostgreSQL, Hosting: AWS EC2 & S3, CDN: CloudFront, Version Control: GitHub
arguments.hardware_requirements: Cloud-based hosting with minimum 2 vCPUs, 8GB RAM for app servers, AWS RDS for database, and 50GB SSD storage; scalable load balancer configuration for peak loads.
arguments.interaction_interface: Responsive web interface with mobile-first design, intuitive navigation, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1), and optimized load times; includes interactive UI elements and seamless database-driven content updates.
arguments.test_optimization_plan: Automated unit and integration testing using Jest and Cypress, load testing with Apache JMeter, continuous performance monitoring via New Relic, and iterative A/B testing for UI/UX refinement.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: Design a modern, scalable website with a modular frontend, API-driven backend, secure authentication, content management, analytics, and extensible service boundaries. Include core components: client application, API gateway/backend services, database layer, caching, file/media storage, logging/monitoring, and deployment pipeline. The architecture should support responsive web experiences, role-based access control, search, and future feature expansion.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: React or Next.js with TypeScript; Backend: Node.js with Express/NestJS or Python with FastAPI; Database: PostgreSQL for relational data, Redis for caching/sessions; Storage: object storage such as S3-compatible service; Authentication: OAuth2/JWT; Infrastructure: Docker, CI/CD, cloud hosting (AWS/Azure/GCP); Observability: centralized logging and metrics with alerting.
args.hardware_requirements: Production-ready cloud infrastructure with autoscaling application instances, managed PostgreSQL, Redis cache, object storage, and CDN. Baseline: 2-4 vCPU app nodes, 8-16 GB RAM per app node, managed database with high availability, scalable storage, and CDN-backed asset delivery. For development: standard laptops or local workstations with 16 GB RAM and Docker support.
args.interaction_interface: Responsive website UI optimized for desktop, tablet, and mobile. Include navigation menu, search, forms, dashboards, and accessible components. Support keyboard navigation, clear error states, and optional personalization features. Provide admin and end-user views where applicable.
args.test_optimization_plan: Implement automated testing across unit, integration, and end-to-end layers. Add performance testing for page load and API latency, accessibility checks (WCAG), security scanning, and cross-browser/device testing. Optimize through CDN usage, image compression, code splitting, caching, database indexing, and continuous monitoring with user feedback loops.
